old_claim.note: Flight (Powered) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from supersonic aerodynamics to powered flight; the node is scoped to shock-wave and compressible-flow science that began before aircraft.

source_shape.source_locator: Chapter 3 discussion of Mach's 1887 shock-wave photographs and the nineteenth-century experimental work on supersonic flow.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NASA describes supersonic-flow research and shock-wave photography in 1887, before powered aircraft existed, and frames it as basic research later used by high-speed aircraft and rockets.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This chronology rejects powered flight as a prerequisite for the scoped scientific node; powered aircraft are downstream applications of supersonic-flow knowledge, not a condition for the first known work.

ontology_before: The graph treated powered flight as an enabling prerequisite for the scientific study of supersonic airflow.
ontology_after: Supersonic aerodynamics is modeled as a pre-aircraft scientific and mathematical foundation that later enabled high-speed aircraft and launch vehicles.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-supersonic-aerodynamics-scope keeps the powered-flight edge absent and locks the 1887 chronology.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to practical aircraft supersonic-flight operations instead of the broader aerodynamics science.
A primary historical source showed that the local node should begin only with powered aircraft wind-tunnel or flight-test programs.
A future graph split moved early shock-wave science into a separate node and reintroduced powered flight only on a later aircraft-operations node.
short_reason: Supersonic-flow science predates powered aircraft, so powered flight is not a prerequisite for the broad aerodynamics node.
